evals.prompt: Design fire and life safety systems for a Tier III enterprise data center with on-site BESS (20MWh lithium-ion, indoor installation). The facility has 4 IT rooms totaling 40,000 sqft, single story. BESS is in a dedicated room adjacent to the electrical switchgear. AHJ is the local fire marshal in Virginia. Facility is 24/7 occupied. Clean agent suppression preferred for IT spaces. 6 x 2.5MW diesel generators with 48-hour fuel storage.
evals.expected_output: A fire/life safety design with VESDA detection in all IT rooms and plenums, Novec 1230 or FM-200 clean agent in IT spaces, NFPA 855 compliant BESS protection (off-gas detection, water-based suppression, deflagration venting, 2-hour fire-rated separation), pre-action sprinkler in support areas, diesel fuel storage SPCC compliance, egress design with emergency lighting and Knox box. BESS spacing requirements quantified and floor area impact noted.
assertions: NFPA 855 compliance is addressed for lithium-ion BESS (thermal runaway, deflagration venting, spacing)
NFPA 75 requirements are addressed for IT room fire protection
VESDA aspirating detection is specified for IT rooms with alarm thresholds
Clean agent suppression (Novec 1230 or FM-200) is specified with design concentration
2-hour fire-rated separation between BESS room and IT spaces is required
BESS spacing requirements (rack-to-rack, rack-to-wall) are quantified

evals.prompt: Design fire and life safety for a hyperscale data center campus with 50+ IT rooms across 5 buildings totaling 500,000 sqft of white space. Tier IV fault-tolerant design. Campus-level fire water distribution loop with dedicated pump house. Each building has 10-12 IT rooms at 5,000 sqft each. No BESS on-site. AHJ is county fire marshal in Oregon. Clean agent preferred. 40 x 3MW diesel generators across the campus. Two-story buildings.
evals.expected_output: A campus-scale fire/life safety design with dedicated fire pump house and campus fire water distribution loop sized for maximum demand. Per-building VESDA systems with campus-level fire alarm monitoring. Clean agent suppression per IT room (independent zones for concurrent maintainability per Tier IV). Pre-action sprinkler in corridors, offices, and mechanical spaces. Diesel generator room protection (flame detection, pre-action sprinkler). Two-story egress design with stairwell pressurization. Fire department access roads per campus layout.
assertions: Campus fire water loop with dedicated pump house is designed for maximum simultaneous demand
VESDA detection is specified per IT room as independent zones
Clean agent suppression zones are independent per room for Tier IV concurrent maintainability
Campus-level fire alarm monitoring system integrates all buildings
Two-story egress design includes stairwell pressurization requirements
Diesel generator room fire protection includes flame detection

evals.prompt: Design fire and life safety for a multi-tenant colocation facility with 30 tenant cages across 3 data halls totaling 60,000 sqft. Tier III. Tenants range from 2 cages to full halls. Some tenants require independent suppression zones for compliance (financial services, healthcare). AHJ requires sprinklers over clean agent (belt-and-suspenders). Facility is occupied 24/7. Single story. No BESS. 8 x 2MW diesel generators.
evals.expected_output: A colo fire/life safety design with tenant-demarcated suppression zones. Clean agent per tenant zone (or per hall for smaller tenants with shared zones). Pre-action sprinkler overlay as required by AHJ (belt-and-suspenders). VESDA per data hall with zone isolation capability. Tenant notification protocol for suppression activation. Suppression system impairment management for tenant work. Cost analysis addressing the belt-and-suspenders requirement doubling installed suppression cost.
assertions: Tenant-demarcated suppression zones are designed for compliance-sensitive tenants
Belt-and-suspenders design (clean agent + sprinkler) addresses AHJ requirement
VESDA zones align with tenant suppression zone boundaries
Tenant notification protocol for suppression activation is specified
Suppression impairment management procedure for tenant work is addressed
Cost impact of belt-and-suspenders dual suppression is quantified
